results.title: Scrimshaw — Wikipedia
results.judge_reason: Source-domain encyclopedia article describes the physical scrimshaw technique but contains nothing about LLM watermarking or parameter-level mechanisms, so it cannot be functionally equivalent to the candidate proposal.

results.title: Carved from the Sea: Nantucket Pictorial Scrimshaw
results.judge_reason: Decorative-arts article describing pin-prick + lampblack-fill scrimshaw technique; pure source-domain reference with no overlap onto LLM watermarking mechanism.

results.title: Scrimshaw Sperm Whale's Tooth, Mid-19th c (Smithsonian)
results.judge_reason: Museum object catalog entry for a single artifact, with minimal technical detail and no connection to LLM watermarking.

results.title: PRO: Precise and Robust Text Watermark (2025/10)
results.judge_reason: Closely related: embeds watermark into 'stable' parameters identified by fine-tuning-induced value changes, conceptually adjacent to the candidate's low-gradient incision framing but uses a stability heuristic rather than explicit recessed-coordinate framing.

results.title: Theoretically Grounded Framework for LLM Watermarking
results.judge_reason: Generic hypothesis-testing watermark framework that does not address fine-tuning robustness or parameter-coordinate selection, so it has weak alignment with the candidate.

results.title: Towards Watermarking of Open-Source LLMs (2025/2)
results.judge_reason: Survey-style positioning paper noting fine-tuning durability as an open-source-LLM watermark requirement; relevant motivation but no recessed-coordinate / incision-fill mechanism.

results.title: Provably Robust Watermarks for Open-Source LMs (2024/10)
results.judge_reason: Per-token bias perturbation watermark with detectability scoring; uses generation-time bias rather than parameter-coordinate weight delta, so mechanism differs from the candidate.

results.title: Robust Data Watermarking by Fictitious Knowledge Injection (2025/3)
results.judge_reason: Training-data memorization watermark explicitly called out as the prior art the candidate distinguishes itself from; mechanism is unrelated to low-gradient parameter incision.
